intent: REWRITE (2026-04-28, after corpus audit). Replaces the previous story 7 ('公園の箱 / The Box in the Park') which had four audited defects: (1) decorative-noun anti-pattern — 鍵 mentioned in s2 then never touched again, (2) empty でも-pivot in s6 (no logical contrast: friend gives book → 'but' I read it), (3) friend with no agency beyond the single transfer, and (4) closer 「私は紙を読みます」 mirrored story 6's closer 「私は本を読みます」 — same `[私]は[N]を読みます` shape. New design retains the box anchor and the nested-container conceit (box → book → paper) — the corpus's first three-level discovery — but rebuilds around real causality. (a) Eliminates the over-anchored 鍵 entirely (it has appeared in stories 1, 3, 5, 6 already; story 7 was its 5th decorative pass). (b) Friend is staged at s0 via companion-と (no teleport) and acts THREE times: takes box, takes book, hands book over. (c) Reflection 「箱は重いです」 matches the era's north-star (「月は白いです。星も白いです。」 — direct attribute description) and adds new tactile information that the visual setting did not establish. (d) Closer 「読む紙は暖かいです」 introduces N5_dictionary_form (G055_plain_nonpast_pair) via a relative clause modifying 紙 — recommended[0] in the brief, priority 13, unlocks 8 downstream points. The closer is also a sensory-recognition beat that threads the warmth motif from stories 3/4 across four stories of corpus continuity. Reinforces all three must-reuse R1 words from story 6 (古い in s4, 本 in s4/s5, 読む in closer) AND G012_soshite_then in s5 (友達は本を取ります。そして、私に渡します。 — preserves the brief's example_surface verbatim). Mints exactly 1 new word (重い — fresh i-adjective, fits the tactile/discovery neighborhood, not minted in stories 8–10 so zero downstream cascade impact). KNOWN downstream debt: G055_plain_nonpast_pair must be reinforced in stories 8/9/10 — to be addressed by adding one relative clause to story 8 (which already has the box/paper continuity).

sentences.jp: 朝、私と友達は公園を歩きます。
sentences.en: In the morning, my friend and I walk through the park.

sentences.jp: 木の下に小さい箱があります。
sentences.en: Under the tree there is a small box.
My friend picks up the box.

sentences.jp: でも、箱の中に古い本があります。
sentences.en: But inside the box there is an old book.

sentences.jp: 友達は本を取ります。そして、私に渡します。
sentences.en: My friend takes the book. Then they hand it to me.

sentences.jp: 本の中に小さい紙があります。
sentences.en: Inside the book there is a small paper.
